creator: pav@FreeBSD.org
text:
> > it's really a dumb idea trying to push libiconv into /usr
> 
> You have to either allow or disallow it. If you allow it, it should  
> work.
> 
> If you are more comfortable with it, think about non-root users  
> installing libiconv. Think about jails, tinybsd or something alike.

Let me rephrase.

It's a dumb idea to install any port into /usr, with an exception of two
ports designed to be installed this way: sendmail and bind.

Of course the system will allow you to install anything anywhere - it's
your system, you're the root, you have the powers. Does not make it less
bad idea.

-- 
Pav Lucistnik <pav@oook.cz>
              <pav@FreeBSD.org>

Eat when you are hungry, sleep when you are tired.
Chase butterflies when you want some fun.
